subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Generates a basic .agripparc.json file, with some default values for options that agrippa accepts

name: generate
gen
subcommands.description: This is the core of the CLI - this command generates a new React component, based on the name and options passed to it

options.name: --props
options.description: Determines which prop declaration/validation solution to generate. Defaults to `ts` if `typescript` flag is `true`, `none` otherwise

options.name: --children
options.description: Whether the component is meant to have children or not. Defaults to `false`

name: --typescript
--ts
options.description: Option to generate Typescript components, defaults to `true` if the CLI manages to find a `tsconfig.json` file, false otherwise

options.name: --import-react
options.description: Option to import React at the top of the component. Defaults to `true` if the CLI manages to find a `tsconfig.json` file and it has a `jsx` field under `compilerOptions` with `react-jsx` or `react-jsxdev` as the value, `false` otherwise

options.name: --export-type
options.description: Whether to export the component as a named export or a default export. Defaults to `named`

options.name: --declaration
options.description: Whether to declare the component as a `const` with an arrow function, or as a `function` declaration. Defaults to `const`

options.name: --memo
options.description: Generates a component as a memo component. Defaults to `false`

options.name: --react-native
options.description: Generates a React Native component. Defaults to `true` if `react-native` is a dependency in the project's `package.json`, `false` otherwise

options.name: --styling
options.description: Which styling solution to generate. Defaults to `none`

options.name: --styling-module
options.description: Whether to generate a scoped `module` stylesheet. This option is only relevant for `css` or `scss` styling, and will be ignored for other values. Defaults to `true`

options.name: --base-dir
options.description: Path to a base directory which components should be generated inside of (directly or nested). Defaults to the current working directory

name: --dest
--destination
options.description: Allows the user to generate the component at a path relative to `baseDir`. It's mostly used to augment or modify the generation path in the command-line when creating the component itself. `dest` is resolved relative to `baseDir`

options.name: --allow-outside-base
options.description: Overrides default Agrippa behaviour to allow components to be specified outside `baseDir`. Defaults to current working directory

options.name: --flat
options.description: By default, Agrippa generates a dedicated directory for the generated component; this directory has the same name as the component, and will contain the component file (as `index.tsx` or `index.jsx`), and the style file (if one is generated). Defaults to `false`

options.name: --separate-index
options.description: In the `separateIndex` generation scheme, Agrippa creates one file for the component's code, and one `index` file, whose purpose is to allow importing exports from the component's directory elegantly (the import path can then be `<path>/<to>/<directory>/Component` instead of `<path>/<to>/<directory>/Component/Component`). Defaults to `true`

name: --post-command
--postCommand
options.description: Allows a custom command to be run after having generated a component. This allows for some cool functionality, such as opening an IDE at the newly generated files automatically or linting them. Defaults to `none`

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: By default, Agrippa prevents the generation of a component over an existing components, to prevent a loss of code. `--overwrite` tells Agrippa to replace existing files, if any are found. Defaults to `false`

options.name: --debug
options.description: The `debug` flag makes Agrippa print out additional debug information. It's quite useful when debugging. Defaults to `false`
